query: What are the minimum educational qualifications required for a capital market intermediary to be appointed?
golden_answer: A professional qualification or post-graduate degree or post graduate diploma (minimum one year in duration) in finance, law, accountancy, business management, commerce, economics, capital market, banking, insurance, actuarial science, fintech, science, technology, engineering or mathematics from a university or an institution recognised by the Central Government or any State Government or a foreign university or institution or association or a CFA or a FRM.

golden_answer: Yes, a clearing member may be permitted to operate in more than one recognized clearing corporation.

query: What happens to existing regulations after the repeal and supersession of new ones?
golden_answer: Notwithstanding such repeal and supersession, -

query: Can a person who gives general comments about trends in the financial or securities market be considered as providing information that requires registration under these regulations?
golden_answer: No, a person who gives general comments in good faith regarding trends in the financial or securities market without specifying any particular securities or investment product does not require registration.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > Inserted by the International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) (Amendment) Regulations, 2026 w.e.f. 12 th January, 2026. > CLAUSE Block

query: Can a person who has been declared as not fit and proper by a regulatory authority still apply for registration?
golden_answer: No, such a person shall not be eligible to apply for any registration until they satisfy the 'fit and proper' requirements.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Global In-House Centres) Regulations, 2025 > PREAMBLE Block > DEFINITION Block > fit and proper

query: What does the term "acceptance of deposits" mean in the context of the International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019?
golden_answer: The acceptance of deposits refers to the act of receiving money or other valuable security from an individual or entity with the understanding that it will be held for safekeeping and returned upon request.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019 > PREAMBLE Block > This Act > DEFINITION Block > CLAUSE Block -> International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019 > PREAMBLE Block > This Act > DEFINITION Block > (ii)

golden_answer: the word 'five'

query: What assistance must CMI give to the inspecting authority during an inspection?
golden_answer: The CMI shall give all assistance as may be required in connection with the inspection.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > PREAMBLE Block > INSPECTION > CLAUSE Block

query: What activities are covered under the term ESG Ratings and Data Products Provider (ERDPP)?
golden_answer: Providing services relating to independent external review for ESG labelled bonds listed on the recognised stock exchanges in an IFSC.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > Notification No. IFSCA/GN/2025/003 dated 11th April 2025 > SCHEDULE Block > DEFINITION Block > ESG Ratings and Data Products Provider (ERDPP)

query: What are the circumstances under which an issuer must provide support for appointing a nominee director by a registered debenture trustee?
golden_answer: In the event of two consecutive defaults in payment of interest or default in creation of security, the issuer shall be obliged to provide all requisite support in this regard, if legally permissible.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > PREAMBLE Block > Custodians > Debenture trustees > (1) > CLAUSE Block -> International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > PREAMBLE Block > Custodians > 4. A registered debenture trustee shall make reasonable efforts to avoid misrepresentation and ensure that the information provided to the debenture holders is not misleading.

query: What happens to references to International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2021 in other regulations after they are repealed?
golden_answer: Any reference thereto in any other regulations made, guidelines or circulars issued thereunder by the Authority shall be deemed to have the reference to the corresponding provisions of these regulations.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Capital Market Intermediaries) Regulations, 2025 > PREAMBLE Block > null > CLAUSE Block > SUBCLAUSE Block

query: What activities can banking units undertake before July 6, 2023?
golden_answer: Banking Units may undertake any or all of the activities mentioned under clause (e) of sub-section (1) of Section 3 of the Act or Section 6 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, except those expressly prohibited by the Authority.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority (Banking) Regulations, 2020 > PREAMBLE Block > OPERATIONAL REQUIREMENTS > Reporting requirements > CLAUSE Block

query: What is the timeframe for laying a copy of every notification proposed under sub-section (1) before each House of Parliament?
golden_answer: A total period of thirty days, which may be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions.
breadcrumb: International Financial Services Centres Authority Act, 2019 > PREAMBLE Block > (b) > (1)

query: What is considered a service recipient under TechFin regulations?
golden_answer: An entity that receives services directly from TechFin and Ancillary Service Provider or through an Intermediary for the purpose of carrying on financial services listed in sub-clauses (i) to (xi) of clause (e) of sub-section (1) of Section 3 of the Act.
breadcrumb: Tech Financing Regulations, 2023 > PREAMBLE Block > CLAUSE Block > DEFINITION Block > Service Recipient
